title: CycloneDX Threat Model
$comment: OWASP CycloneDX is an Ecma International standard (ECMA-424) developed in collaboration between the OWASP Foundation and Ecma Technical Committee 54 (TC54). The standard is published under a royalty-free patent policy. This JSON schema is the reference implementation and is licensed under the Apache License 2.0.

threats.title: Threats
threats.description: Threat-modelling content, including the documented threats, the scenarios that realize them, the attack patterns and attack trees that describe how they are carried out, the trust boundaries they cross, and the security policies that govern the system.

threats.title: Threats
threats.description: The documented threats. Each describes what can go wrong, independent of any specific actor or occurrence.

scenarios.title: Scenarios
scenarios.description: The threat scenarios, each realizing one or more documented threats in a specific way.

attackPatterns.title: Attack Patterns
attackPatterns.description: A library of attack patterns, typically aligned with CAPEC, that threats and scenarios can reference.

attackTrees.title: Attack Trees
attackTrees.description: Attack trees that decompose attacker goals into sub-goals and techniques.

abuseCases.title: Abuse Cases
abuseCases.description: Abuse or misuse cases that describe how the system can be exercised by an adversary.

trustBoundaries.title: Trust Boundaries
trustBoundaries.description: The trust boundaries of the system, extending the architectural boundaries defined in a blueprint with trust semantics.

methodologies.title: Methodologies
methodologies.description: The threat modeling methodologies applied to produce this threat model, such as STRIDE, LINDDUN, PASTA, or attack trees.

methodology.title: Threat Modeling Methodology
methodology.description: A threat modeling methodology applied to produce this threat model. Use the custom option for methodologies not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.STRIDE: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege.
meta:enum.LINDDUN: Linkability, Identifiability, Non-repudiation, Detectability, Disclosure of information, Unawareness, Non-compliance.
meta:enum.PASTA: Process for Attack Simulation and Threat Analysis.
meta:enum.MAESTRO: Multi-Agent Environment, Security, Threat, Risk, and Outcome.
meta:enum.VAST: Visual, Agile, and Simple Threat modelling.
meta:enum.ATFAA: Advanced Threat Framework for Autonomous AI Agents.
meta:enum.attack-tree: Attack tree methodology, decomposing attacker goals into sub-goals and techniques.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom threat modeling methodology.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom threat modeling methodology.

threat.title: Threat
threat.description: A documented threat, describing what can go wrong independent of any specific actor or occurrence. A threat can be catalogued on its own; a threat scenario references it to describe a specific realization.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the threat elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

source.title: Source
source.description: The source of the threat, such as a threat intelligence feed, a threat catalogue, or an analysis activity.

origin.title: Origin
origin.description: The nature of the threat source that gives rise to this threat, following the threat source types of [NIST SP 800-30](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/30/r1/final). Use the custom option for an origin not covered by the enumeration.

meta:enum.adversarial: An adversary acting with intent, such as an individual, group, organization, or nation state.
meta:enum.accidental: An erroneous action taken without malicious intent, such as a mistake by a privileged user or administrator.
meta:enum.structural: A failure of equipment, software, or environmental controls, such as resource exhaustion or expiry of a component.
meta:enum.environmental: A natural or human-made event outside the control of the organization, such as a fire, flood, or wide-area infrastructure outage.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the custom origin.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the custom origin.

categories.title: Categories
categories.description: The methodology-specific categories that this threat falls under. A threat may be categorized under more than one methodology, for example STRIDE and MAESTRO. The methodology is one of the supported threat-modelling methodologies, and it determines which category values are permitted.

items.title: Threat Category
items.description: A threat category. The taxonomy determines the permitted category values.

taxonomy.title: Taxonomy
taxonomy.description: The threat classification taxonomy that the category is drawn from.

meta:enum.STRIDE: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, and Elevation of Privilege.
meta:enum.LINDDUN: Linkability, Identifiability, Non-repudiation, Detectability, Disclosure of information, Unawareness, and Non-compliance.
meta:enum.MAESTRO: Multi-Agent Environment, Security, Threat, Risk, and Outcome, whose categories are its seven architectural layers.
meta:enum.MITRE-ATTACK: The MITRE ATT&CK framework, whose categories are its Enterprise tactics.

category.title: Category
category.description: The category within the taxonomy. The permitted values are determined by the taxonomy.

meta:enum.spoofing: Impersonating something or someone, violating authenticity.
meta:enum.tampering: Modifying data or code, violating integrity.
meta:enum.repudiation: Denying having performed an action, violating non-repudiation.
meta:enum.information-disclosure: Exposing information to unauthorized parties, violating confidentiality.
meta:enum.denial-of-service: Denying or degrading service to legitimate users, violating availability.
meta:enum.elevation-of-privilege: Gaining capabilities without proper authorization, violating authorization.

meta:enum.linkability: Linking data items to learn more about an individual.
meta:enum.non-repudiation: Being unable to deny a claim, harming plausible deniability.
meta:enum.detectability: Detecting that an item of interest exists.
meta:enum.unawareness: Individuals being unaware of the processing of their data.
meta:enum.non-compliance: Deviating from policy, regulation, or best practice.

meta:enum.foundation-models: Layer 1. The core models that provide the agent's capabilities.
meta:enum.data-operations: Layer 2. Storage, retrieval, and processing of the agent's data.
meta:enum.agent-frameworks: Layer 3. The frameworks and tools that build and run agents.
meta:enum.deployment-and-infrastructure: Layer 4. The infrastructure on which agents are deployed and scaled.
meta:enum.evaluation-and-observability: Layer 5. Evaluation, monitoring, and observability of agent behaviour.
meta:enum.security-and-compliance: Layer 6. The cross-cutting security and compliance controls.
meta:enum.agent-ecosystem: Layer 7. The applications and marketplace where agents deliver value to end-users.

weaknesses.description: The weaknesses, such as CWE classifications, that this threat exploits.

attackPatterns.title: Attack Patterns
attackPatterns.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the attack patterns by which this threat is carried out.

attackTrees.title: Attack Trees
attackTrees.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the attack trees that decompose this threat.

abuseCases.title: Abuse Cases
abuseCases.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the abuse cases that illustrate how this threat can be exercised.

killChainPhase.title: Kill Chain Phase
killChainPhase.description: The phase of the cyber kill chain at which this threat operates.

meta:enum.delivery: Transmitting the payload to the target.
meta:enum.installation: Installing malicious software or establishing a foothold.
meta:enum.command-and-control: Establishing a command and control channel.
meta:enum.actions-on-objectives: Achieving the goals of the attack.

behaviors.title: Behaviors
behaviors.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the declared behaviors that constitute the intent of this threat, drawn from the behavior taxonomy.

affectedAssets.title: Affected Assets
affectedAssets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the kinds of assets this threat affects.

mitigations.title: Mitigations
mitigations.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that mitigate this threat.

threatScenario.title: Threat Scenario
threatScenario.description: A specific realization of a threat, in which an actor seeks to cause harm. The scenario references the threat it realizes, supplies the actor by reference to a party, and carries the scenario-specific traits and risk assessment, such as motivation, intent, access level, likelihood, and impact.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the threat scenario elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the threat scenario.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the threat scenario.

threats.title: Threats
threats.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the threats that this scenario realizes. A scenario realizes one or more threats.

actor.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the party that is the actor in this scenario. Named adversary groups are organizations and abstract attacker classes are personas.

threatProfile.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to a reusable threat profile that describes the durable capabilities of the actor.

motivation.title: Motivation
motivation.description: The motivations of the actor in this scenario.

meta:enum.destruction: Motivated by the desire to destroy.
meta:enum.disruption: Motivated by the desire to disrupt operations.
meta:enum.reputation: Motivated by reputational gain or harm.

intent.title: Intent
intent.description: The intent of the actor in this scenario.

meta:enum.accidental: The harm is caused without intent.
meta:enum.opportunistic: The actor takes advantage of an opportunity as it arises.
meta:enum.targeted: The actor deliberately targets the subject.
meta:enum.persistent: The actor pursues the target persistently over time.

accessLevel.title: Access Level
accessLevel.description: The level of access the actor is assumed to have in this scenario.

meta:enum.internal: Internal access, such as that of an ordinary user.

riskAttributes.title: Risk Attributes
riskAttributes.description: The security and privacy attributes that this threat would compromise if realized.

affectedAssets.title: Affected Assets
affectedAssets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the assets affected in this scenario.

relatedRisks.title: Related Risks
relatedRisks.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the risks that this scenario contributes to.

threatProfiles.title: Threat Profiles
threatProfiles.description: Reusable threat profiles, each describing the durable characteristics of an actor that may pose a threat.

threatProfile.title: Threat Profile
threatProfile.description: A durable, actor-level characterization of the capability to pose a threat, capturing attributes that persist across scenarios. Scenario-specific attributes, such as motivation, intent, and access level, belong on a threat scenario rather than on this profile.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference this threat profile elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the threat profile.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the threat profile.

sophistication.title: Sophistication
sophistication.description: The level of technical sophistication an actor characterized by this profile is assumed to possess.

meta:enum.minimal: Minimal sophistication, relying on readily available tools and techniques.
meta:enum.advanced: Advanced skills consistent with a persistent and well-resourced actor.
meta:enum.expert: Expert skills consistent with the most capable actors.

resources.title: Resources
resources.description: The level of resources an actor characterized by this profile is assumed to have available.

skillSet.title: Skill Set
skillSet.description: The specific skills an actor characterized by this profile is assumed to possess.

attackVector.title: Attack Vector
attackVector.description: The conditions under which an attack is possible, following the convention of common vulnerability scoring systems.

type.title: Type
type.description: The context by which an attack is possible.

privileges.title: Privileges
privileges.description: The level of privileges required to carry out the attack.

userInteraction.title: User Interaction
userInteraction.description: Whether the attack requires interaction from a user other than the attacker.

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: Whether a successful attack affects resources beyond the vulnerable component.

meta:enum.unchanged: The attack affects only the vulnerable component.
meta:enum.changed: The attack affects resources beyond the vulnerable component.

attackPattern.title: Attack Pattern
attackPattern.description: A description of the common attributes and approach of an attack, typically aligned with CAPEC. Techniques are aligned with MITRE ATT&CK.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the attack pattern elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

capecId.title: CAPEC Identifier
capecId.description: The Common Attack Pattern Enumeration and Classification (CAPEC) identifier for this attack pattern.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the attack pattern.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the attack pattern.

prerequisites.title: Prerequisites
prerequisites.description: The conditions that must hold for the attack to be possible.

techniques.title: Techniques
techniques.description: The techniques used to carry out the attack, typically aligned with MITRE ATT&CK.

mitigations.title: Mitigations
mitigations.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that mitigate this attack pattern.

examples.title: Examples
examples.description: Real-world examples of the attack pattern.

technique.title: Technique
technique.description: A specific technique used to carry out an attack, typically aligned with MITRE ATT&CK.

id.title: Identifier
id.description: The identifier of the technique, such as a MITRE ATT&CK technique identifier.

tactic.title: Tactic
tactic.description: The tactic that the technique supports, such as a MITRE ATT&CK tactic.

procedure.title: Procedure
procedure.description: The specific procedure by which the technique is carried out.

attackTree.title: Attack Tree
attackTree.description: A decomposition of an attacker goal into sub-goals and techniques, expressed as a flat collection of nodes. Each node lists its child nodes by reference together with an operator that says how those children combine, so the tree is expressed without nesting. One node is the root, representing the attacker goal.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the attack tree elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the attack tree.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the attack tree.

root.title: Root BOM Reference
root.description: Optional reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the root node within nodes, representing the attacker goal. When omitted, the root is the node that no other node lists as a child.

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.description: The nodes of the attack tree. Each node references its child nodes, which are themselves entries in this array, forming the hierarchy without nesting.

attackTreeNode.title: Attack Tree Node
attackTreeNode.description: A node in an attack tree, representing an attacker goal, sub-goal, or technique. A node references its child nodes and carries an operator that says how those children combine. The hierarchy is expressed by these references rather than by nesting.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the node elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The goal, sub-goal, or technique that the node represents.

operator.title: Operator
operator.description: Specifies how the nodes listed in 'children' combine to achieve this node. A value of 'and' requires every child node; a value of 'or' requires any single child node. For example, a node whose children are a phishing node and a credential-stuffing node uses 'or' when either alone achieves it, or 'and' when both are needed together. A node with no children is a leaf and omits this property.

meta:enum.and: All child nodes are required to achieve this node.
meta:enum.or: Any one child node is sufficient to achieve this node.

children.title: Children
children.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the child nodes that decompose this node. Each child node is a separate entry in the same nodes array, so the hierarchy is expressed without nesting. A child node may be shared by more than one parent.

attackPattern.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the attack pattern that this node represents.

weakness.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the weakness that this node exploits.

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: The relative cost to an attacker of achieving this node.

skill.title: Skill
skill.description: The skill level required to achieve this node.

detectability.title: Detectability
detectability.description: How readily an attempt to achieve this node can be detected.

meta:enum.low: The attempt is difficult to detect.

mitigations.title: Mitigations
mitigations.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that mitigate this node.

abuseCase.title: Abuse Case
abuseCase.description: A description of how an adversary can misuse the system to achieve a malicious goal. An abuse case is the security counterpart of a use case and reuses its flow structure.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the abuse case elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the abuse case, typically expressed as a malicious goal.

description.title: Description
description.description: A narrative describing how the system can be misused or abused.

abuser.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the party that abuses the system, typically an attacker persona.

realizes.title: Realizes
realizes.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the threats that this abuse case realizes.

preconditions.title: Preconditions
preconditions.description: The conditions that must hold before the abuse case can begin.

mainFlow.title: Main Flow
mainFlow.description: The ordered steps the abuser follows.

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the assets or flows targeted in this abuse case.

detectionOpportunities.title: Detection Opportunities
detectionOpportunities.description: The signals, logs, or controls that present an opportunity to detect the abuse.

mitigations.title: Mitigations
mitigations.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls that mitigate this abuse case.

exploitability.title: Exploitability
exploitability.description: The degree to which a threat can be exploited in practice.

level.title: Level
level.description: The maturity of available exploitation capability.

meta:enum.theoretical: Exploitation is theoretically possible but has not been demonstrated.
meta:enum.weaponized: A weaponized exploit is in use.

skillRequired.title: Skill Required
skillRequired.description: The skill level required to exploit the threat.

indicators.title: Indicators
indicators.description: The observable indicators associated with a threat.

compromise.title: Indicators of Compromise
compromise.description: The indicators that a compromise has occurred.

attack.title: Indicators of Attack
attack.description: The indicators that an attack is under way.

signatures.title: Signatures
signatures.description: The detection signatures associated with the threat.

signature.title: Signature
signature.description: A detection signature for a threat.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of what the signature detects.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: The confidence level of a detection by this signature.

trustBoundary.title: Trust Boundary
trustBoundary.description: A security annotation marking an architectural boundary as one across which trust changes. It references a boundary defined in a blueprint, where the zones, type, and crossing requirements already live, and adds only the trust differential and the threats and controls at that boundary.

bom-ref.description: An identifier which can be used to reference the trust boundary elsewhere using a bom-ref or bom-link.

boundary.description: Reference using bom-link or bom-ref to the blueprint boundary that this trust boundary annotates.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the trust boundary.

description.title: Description
description.description: A description of the trust boundary.

trustLevel.title: Trust Level
trustLevel.description: The trust level differential across the boundary.

meta:enum.untrusted: The boundary separates an untrusted zone.
meta:enum.semi-trusted: The boundary separates a semi-trusted zone.
meta:enum.trusted: The boundary separates a trusted zone.
meta:enum.highly-trusted: The boundary separates a highly trusted zone.

threatsAtBoundary.title: Threats At Boundary
threatsAtBoundary.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the threats that could exploit this boundary.

controlsAtBoundary.title: Controls At Boundary
controlsAtBoundary.description: References using bom-link or bom-ref to the controls implemented at this boundary.
